articles.title: Analysis: Fed Chairman Warsh faces an inflation credibility test after Congress hearings
articles.description: The new Fed chairman avoided major stumbles in two days of testimony before the House and Senate, but faces a rapid test of his commitment to price stability.

articles.title: Trump Faces the Limits of U.S. Firepower and the Lessons of Past Wars
articles.description: Like his predecessors, President Trump has struggled to turn battlefield successes into long-term victories.

articles.title: Oil Prices Rise to Highest Level in a Month as Shipping in the Persian Gulf Nears a Halt
articles.description: Shipping traffic was at its lowest level in more than a month and oil prices moved higher as the Iran war continued to disrupt the supply of energy.

articles.title: Trump rips Canada as wildfire smoke spreads, says U.S. will add pollution cost to tariffs
articles.description: The smoke coming from active wildfires in Canada has led to unhealthy air quality in swaths of the U.S., which could affect the World Cup final in New Jersey.

articles.description: Gold heads for biggest weekly loss in six as Middle East war fans inflation worries CNBC

articles.title: First Patient Dies of Legionnaires’ Disease After New York Outbreak
articles.description: New York City’s Health Department is still seeking to identify the source of an outbreak on the Upper East Side that has infected 67 people.

articles.description: Opinion | The Iran war is a gift. Just not for the United States. The Washington Post

articles.title: 7 Americans Sent to Disputed Kenya Ebola Site After New Trump Travel Ban
articles.description: The aid workers were on the frontline in the fight against the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and have shown no symptoms, their employer said.

articles.title: War Between U.S. and Iran Expands, With Strikes Across the Region
articles.description: Videos and reports in Iranian state media showed damage to bridges, railways and other infrastructure. U.S. allies in the region reported retaliatory strikes by Iran.

articles.title: The 20-Somethings Running Ukraine’s War Machine
articles.description: A new generation has been cutting red tape to speed weapons to the front. But the old guard pushed back, and now Ukraine’s young defense minister is out.

articles.title: 7.3-Magnitude Earthquake Off Coast of Mexico and Guatemala Poses Tsunami Threat
articles.description: A powerful earthquake near the border of Mexico and Guatemala raised fears of a tsunami and shook buildings across three countries.

articles.title: Israeli Strike in Gaza Kills Seven People, Health Officials Say
articles.description: The Israeli military said the attack targeted members of the second-biggest militant group in Gaza. It also said it was “aware of the claims that several uninvolved individuals” had been harmed.

articles.title: Iran-U.S. Talks: How 4 Negotiators Would Approach Diplomacy
articles.description: First step: Agree on a goal.

articles.title: Beijing Rejects Trump’s Claim of Meddling, but Avoids a Fight
articles.description: Chinese officials called the election-meddling accusation baseless, while analysts said Beijing was trying to preserve a fragile détente.

articles.title: Cooler Inflation May Not Be Enough to Stave Off Fed Rate Increases
articles.description: The Federal Reserve is expected to hold rates steady at its July meeting, but officials have not written off the possibility of a move soon after.

articles.title: Democratic socialists top MAGA candidates among voters in CNBC's All-America poll
articles.description: The survey's findings come as democratic socialist candidates win Democratic primaries following the 2025 election of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

articles.title: Firms With Checkered Pasts Tied to Troubled Midtown Construction Project
articles.description: Among those connected to companies that worked on 235 East 42nd Street were a man who once bribed city officials and a developer who gave a loan to Bernard Kerik.

articles.title: Xi Jinping of China Pitches ‘Openness’ in Push to Shape the Path of A.I.
articles.description: Xi Jinping said the development of artificial intelligence systems should be a “symphony of global collaboration.”

articles.title: Cyclospora Investigation Turns to Farms in Mexico
articles.description: The F.D.A. is planning to inspect farms and a lettuce shredding facility to determine how lettuce supplied by the produce company Taylor Farms to Taco Bell became contaminated and where it was shipped.

articles.title: Lam Wing-Kee, Hong Kong Bookseller Who Defied Authorities, Dies at 70
articles.description: One of five booksellers kidnapped by Chinese officials in 2015, Mr. Lam spoke out after his release and became an international cause célèbre.

articles.title: Karim Khan’s Accuser Breaks Silence on Alleged Sexual Misconduct at I.C.C.
articles.description: Karim Khan, the chief prosecutor at the International Criminal Court, has denied the accusations. He faces a vote next week that will determine whether he keeps his job.

articles.title: Big Tech Firms Like Oracle Turn to Bonds to Finance A.I. Data Centers
articles.description: Risk is rising as big tech companies like Oracle — the ultimate financial source of the Ellison media empire — need to turn to the bond market for staggering sums to finance data centers.

articles.title: Stocks Sink on Anxiety About Tech and A.I. Spending
articles.description: Investors are growing uneasy about increasing competition from China in the global race to dominate artificial intelligence.

articles.title: China’s Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi Model, Threatening America’s Lead
articles.description: China’s Moonshot AI unveiled a freely available artificial intelligence model that seemed to narrow the gap with cutting-edge offerings from U.S. tech companies.

articles.title: Cuban Dissident Was Missing A Week After Prison Release
articles.description: A Cuban artist awaiting permission from the Trump administration to enter the United States was held in an unknown location in Cuba.

articles.title: Prominent Cuban Artist Will Go Into Exile After 5-Year Prison Term
articles.description: The United States offered a visa to Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, who was expected to fly to Miami on Saturday.

articles.title: Burnham Becomes Labour Leader and Britain’s Incoming Prime Minister
articles.description: At a time of political upheaval and economic stagnation, Andy Burnham will on Monday become the seventh prime minister in a decade.

articles.title: X and Music Publishers Retreat From Lawsuits
articles.description: Elon Musk’s social media platform and the music companies agreed to dismiss dueling claims, from 2023 and 2026. They have not said why.

articles.title: Poolhouse, a Tech-Infused Pool Hall Start-up, Raises $55 Million
articles.description: Poolhouse, which was created by the founders of Topgolf and uses technology to track players’ scores, aims to sell its platform to others.

articles.title: Pentagon’s Escort Policy for Journalists Is Temporarily Restored
articles.description: An appeals court said the Defense Department could require escorts for reporters who visit the Pentagon while The New York Times sues to overturn the rule.

articles.title: America’s Enterprising Spirit Is Booming After Decades-Long Slump
articles.description: Hundreds of thousands of people are creating companies each year, ending a decades-long drought.

articles.title: TSMC Adds $100 Billion to Its U.S. Spending Plan
articles.description: The pledge from the Taiwanese chip-making giant brought the total commitment to its fast-growing operations in Arizona to $265 billion.

articles.title: Christopher Nolan on ‘The Odyssey’ and IMAX Technical Challenges
articles.description: He and the cinematographer Hoyte van Hoytema combined new inventions (like fire-mimicking lights they developed) with century-old techniques.

articles.title: Parents in Uganda Demand Answers After Bus Crash Kills 21 Children
articles.description: The driver of the school bus, carrying dozens of students, lost control, officials said, causing the bus to veer off the road into a boulder.

articles.title: E.U. Proposes Changes to Emissions Trading System
articles.description: The European Union is facing a green backlash as emissions cuts become harder and require bigger overhauls to industry and livelihoods

articles.title: When a World Cup Team Needs a Trim, the Barbershop Comes to Them
articles.description: Maram Hammadi, who grew up playing on dirt fields in Iraq before opening a barbershop outside Seattle, heard Egypt needed a barber. He cleared his books.

articles.title: ‘Blue MAGA’ Has Entered the Chat
articles.description: It’s a term that can mean whatever the people using it want it to mean. But it’s not a compliment.

articles.title: Hundreds of Rohingya Feared Drowned at Sea
articles.description: Members of the persecuted minority group from Myanmar set off in search of a better life weeks ago, but news of their boats sinking only emerged recently. More than 500 are feared dead.

articles.title: New Prime Minister Faces Old Problems: How to Make Britain’s Economy Grow
articles.description: Conversations with economists, and people around him, shed light on how Andy Burnham might tackle entrenched challenges.

articles.title: A New Blow to Tracking Gun Sales
articles.description: The House has passed a bill that would bar the use of merchant codes specifically for firearm retailers, an effort to pre-empt state adoption of the tool.
